summary: Iteration 50 — Marketing push for 'Book Now · Pay After Ride' verified across hero, fleet cards, landing pages, booking form, pay page, and Stripe checkout (submit_type + custom_text). Also RouteMap short-trip render + iter49 regression. All acceptance criteria PASS. 11/11 pytest + 100% frontend UI checks.

hero_pay_after_badge_and_cta.evidence: data-testid=hero-pay-after-badge present, text 'Book Now · Pay After Your Ride · No card charged today'. hero-cta-book text = 'Reserve Now · Pay After Ride'.

fleet_ribbons_eligible_vehicles.evidence: pay-after-ribbon-{Executive Sedan|First Class|Luxury SUV} all present with 'BOOK NOW · PAY AFTER RIDE' text (screenshot verified). Ribbon hidden when the card is selected (by design).

fleet_ribbons_group_vehicles_hidden.evidence: pay-after-ribbon-{Party Bus|Motor Coach|Sprinter|Stretch Limousine|Jet Sprinter|Mini Coach|Executive Sprinter} — none rendered.

booking_form_pay_timing_order.evidence: DOM order = [pay-timing-block, pay-timing-after, pay-timing-now]. 'after' comes first.

booking_form_pay_timing_after_recommende.evidence: pay-timing-after button contains 'Recommended' + 'Book Now · Pay After Ride' + '$0 today' gold badge + 'Card securely verified & saved by Stripe' copy.

booking_form_submit_copy_after.evidence: After clicking pay-timing-after: submit text = 'Reserve Now · $0 Due Today →'.

booking_form_submit_copy_now.evidence: After clicking pay-timing-now: submit text = 'Secure Checkout · $178.20 →'.

booking_trust_payment_badges.evidence: data-testid=booking-trust-badges renders below submit row when a priced vehicle is picked. wallet-badges + all 5 wallet marks (Apple Pay/Google Pay/Visa/Mastercard/American Express) present.

google_review_badge_no_count.evidence: google-review-badge shows 5 stars + text '5-Star Google Reviews' — NO review count / rating number / 'based on' phrasing (screenshot verified).

paybooking_trust_badges.evidence: Fresh unpaid booking at /pay/{id}: 'Pay $178.27 & Secure' button + data-testid=pay-page-trust-badges renders below it with all 5 wallet marks + google-review-badge '5-Star Google Reviews'.

backend_checkout_pay_now_submit_type_cus.evidence: POST /api/payments/checkout with submit_type=book + custom_text.submit.message + product description succeeds (200) and returns https://checkout.stripe.com/ URL. Stripe accepted the new params (would 502 if not). Verified via test_iter50_marketing_push.py::TestCheckoutPayNow (2/2 pass).

backend_checkout_setup_custom_text.evidence: POST /api/payments/checkout-setup with custom_text still creates a valid setup session; booking flipped to pay_after_ride. Verified via TestCheckoutSetupCustomText + all iter49 tests still passing.

route_map_short_trip.evidence: SFO → Palo Alto: route-map + route-distance='21.9 mi' rendered, no route-error. _computeRoute both boots on map-ready AND on address change; JS `const` hoisting inside function scope means callback closure resolves at effect execution time (safe).

iter49_regression.evidence: All 8 existing iter49 pytest cases still pass (checkout-setup happy path, rejection, guards, public booking fields, admin charge-pay-later auth/guards). Full 11/11 combined suite pass in 8.64s.

action_items: OPTIONAL UX consideration (not a bug, matches spec literally): BookingForm's default payTiming state is 'now' (BookingForm.jsx:127 `useState('now')`). Given this iteration's entire goal is to nudge users to pay-after-ride (the recommended, higher-conversion path), consider defaulting to 'after' so first-time visitors see the '$0 Due Today' submit label without needing to click. The 'Recommended' badge helps, but defaults win. Not blocking — flagging for main-agent product decision.
critical_code_review_comments: BookingForm.jsx is now ~1505 lines (up from 1400 last iter). Same recommendation as iter49: extract PayTimingBlock, TrustBadgesRow, PromoSection into child components (>700 line guideline).

RouteMap.jsx line 66-106 useEffect uses `_computeRoute` (declared below at line 111). Works at runtime because JS `const` bindings in the same function scope are initialized before the useEffect callback fires (callback runs post-render). But react-hooks/exhaustive-deps lint would complain — consider using `useCallback` for _computeRoute or defining it above the boot effect. Not a bug now, but risk of TDZ issues if refactored to fire the effect synchronously.

RouteMap.jsx line 106 dep-array is `[apiKey, pickup, dropoff]` — missing `stops`. `stops` change won't trigger a re-boot (harmless since boot only runs once via mapInstanceRef guard) but the address-change effect (line 154-156) does include stops.

TrustPaymentBadges Mastercard SVG has overlapping circles (correct brand). Visa, Amex marks use plain SVG text (no license risk). Google Pay uses the multi-color 'G' logo — verify with legal that this rendering is compliant with Google Pay brand guidelines.

payments.py checkout line 199 sets submit_type=book — good. Line 205 lists 'Apple Pay & Google Pay accepted' in the submit message text — this is factually correct because Stripe auto-enables wallets when payment_method_types=[card] and the browser supports them; no additional server config needed.
test_credentials: Admin: support@turanelitelimo.com / TuronAdmin@2025 (not exercised — no admin UI in scope this iter).
seed_data_creation: One TEST_ pay-now booking created for /pay/{id} UI verification: id=4dcfca3e-2153-48d6-b81a-8e5f2bafdd05, email=iter50-paybooking+*. Plus multiple TEST_ pay-later bookings via the pytest suite (email prefix 'paylater-test+' and 'iter50-test+'). All seed rows use future date 2026-08-15 and are safe to leave.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 50 marketing push fully verified. New backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iter50_marketing_push.py (3 cases) — reuses booking-create helper pattern from iter49. Stripe is LIVE — never enter card details. The BookingForm default payTiming='now' matches literal spec but is flagged as UX consideration (see action_items). RouteMap short trip (SFO → Palo Alto = 21.9 mi) renders cleanly; the extracted _computeRoute pattern works — race condition fix confirmed by design review, no observable failure in browser test. Frontend testId reminder: pickup/dropoff inputs are 'booking-pickup' and 'booking-dropoff' (NOT '-input' suffix); vehicle cards are 'vehicle-card-{Name}'. Use page.keyboard.type() to trigger React state update on autocomplete inputs, not element.fill().
